summary: Messaging Channels feature fully tested. Backend: 19/19 API tests passed - all CRUD operations work correctly. Frontend: Settings page loads with all 7 channel cards, toggle switches visible, preview section shows single/multi-channel mode messaging. Menu item appears in Administration section with WhatsApp green icon.
critical_code_review_comments: messaging_channels.py: Well-structured API with 7 channels - SMS, WhatsApp, Messenger, Telegram, LinkedIn, Email, Clipboard
messaging_channels.py: GET /available returns all channels with metadata (id, name, icon, color, description, url_scheme, requires_phone)
messaging_channels.py: GET/PUT /org/{org_id} correctly validates org_id format and returns 400/404 appropriately
messaging_channels.py: GET /user/{user_id} properly falls back to org config and returns channel metadata
messaging_channels.py: PUT validates invalid channel IDs return 400 error
messaging-channels.tsx: Settings page shows all 7 channel cards with toggle switches
messaging-channels.tsx: Info banner text changes based on enabled count (single vs multi-channel)
messaging-channels.tsx: Preview section shows 'opens directly' for 1 channel, picker preview for multiple
messaging-channels.tsx: At least 1 channel must stay enabled (frontend validation)
ChannelPicker.tsx: Auto-sends when only 1 channel enabled (skips modal), shows picker for multiple
more.tsx line 534: Messaging Channels menu item with chatbubbles icon and WhatsApp green (#25D366)
contact/[id].tsx: ChannelPicker component integrated at lines 4677-4685, called via channelPicker hook at line 1376

success_rate.frontend: 100% - All UI features verified via Playwright

seed_data_creation: No seed data created. Used existing org config during tests, restored original state after each test.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Messaging Channels feature complete. Backend has 4 endpoints: GET /api/messaging-channels/available (returns all 7 channels), GET /api/messaging-channels/org/{org_id} (returns enabled channels for org), PUT /api/messaging-channels/org/{org_id} (updates enabled channels), GET /api/messaging-channels/user/{user_id} (returns user's enabled channels based on org config). Frontend Settings > Messaging Channels page accessible via More menu > Administration section. Channel toggles use react-native Switch component. ChannelPicker component integrated into contact detail page's message composer - auto-sends when 1 channel enabled, shows picker modal for multiple channels.

features_verified.backend_get_available: PASS - Returns all 7 channels with correct metadata
features_verified.backend_get_org_channels: PASS - Returns org config with enabled_channels and available list
features_verified.backend_put_org_channels: PASS - Updates enabled channels, validates channel IDs
features_verified.backend_get_user_channels: PASS - Returns user's channels based on org config with full metadata
features_verified.backend_invalid_channel_validation: PASS - Returns 400 for invalid channel IDs
features_verified.backend_invalid_org_validation: PASS - Returns 400 for invalid org ID format
features_verified.backend_invalid_user_validation: PASS - Returns 400 for invalid user ID format
features_verified.frontend_settings_page_loads: PASS - All 7 channel cards visible with icons and descriptions
features_verified.frontend_preview_single_mode: PASS - Shows 'Messages open directly in SMS / iMessage'
features_verified.frontend_info_banner: PASS - Shows 'With one channel enabled, messages open directly'
features_verified.frontend_menu_item: PASS - Messaging Channels in Administration section with WhatsApp green icon
features_verified.frontend_channel_picker_integration: PASS - ChannelPicker component rendered in contact detail page
